Control Arm.value: Placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Control Arm.reasoning: In the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The darolutamide group is the experimental arm, making the 'placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel' the control arm.

Number of Arms Included.reasoning: The 'METHODS' section states that patients were randomly assigned to 'darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. The CONSORT flow diagram (FIG 1) further details these two arms: 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' and 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT', indicating two arms in total.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily, in combination with ADT and docetaxel
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.reasoning: The 'METHODS' section under 'Trial Design' states: 'Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel.' This describes the specific treatment regimen for the darolutamide arm (Treatment Arm 1).

Treatment Arm - N.reasoning: The CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1) states that 1,306 patients were randomly assigned. The 'Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT' arm, which is the experimental arm, had n = 651 participants.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Treatment Arm(s).reasoning: The 'METHODS' section (2e750784-9881-4b5e-8c78-9a2394ec3a6d) states: 'Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.' The 'INTRODUCTION' section (1d2b6194-e112-4872-a1e2-3e8cf6168f2a) further clarifies: 'The darolutamide group had a significant reduction in the risk of death by 32.5% (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.80; P < .0001) compared with the placebo group.' This indicates 'darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel' as the experimental arm.

Total Participants - N.reasoning: In the 'RESULTS' section under 'Patient Characteristics', it is stated: 'As previously reported, a total of 1,306 patients were randomly assigned, and 1,305 patients were included in the full analysis set of ARASENS (Fig 1).' The question asks for participants included in the clinical trial, which refers to the analysis set.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).reasoning: The document reports median survival and median treatment duration, but does not explicitly state the 'Median Follow-Up Duration (mo)' for the study participants.

Quality of Life reported.reasoning: The 'Study Assessments' section on page 3 states that secondary efficacy end points included 'time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire' and 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy–Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire', which are measures related to quality of life.

Control Arm - N.reasoning: The value is found in Figure 1, 'CONSORT flow diagram', under 'Patients randomly assigned (N = 1,306)', where it states 'Placebo + docetaxel + ADT (n = 655)'.

Trial.reasoning: The name of the clinical study is explicitly mentioned in the title of the document: 'Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial'.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.reasoning: The 'INTRODUCTION' section states that 'darolutamide' is an 'androgen receptor pathway inhibitor' and that the ARASENS trial evaluated the combination of ADT, docetaxel, and an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (darolutamide). The 'DISCUSSION' section further confirms this by stating 'Results of ARASENS indicate an advantage to adding an androgen receptor inhibitor (darolutamide) to ADT and docetaxel'.

Quality of Life Scale.value: Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire; Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy–Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire
Quality of Life Scale.reasoning: On page 3, under the 'Study Assessments' section, the document lists secondary efficacy end points including 'time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire' and 'time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy–Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire'. These are standardized questionnaires used to assess aspects of the participants' well-being and perceived quality of life.

NCT.reasoning: Found under 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' on page 12, section 18ce4502-417b-485a-ae4a-7dd698b539b9.

PubMed ID.reasoning: The document provides a DOI (https://doi.org/10.1200/JC0.23.00041) but does not explicitly state a PubMed ID for this specific publication.

Trial Name.value: Darolutamide Plus Androgen-Deprivation Therapy and Docetaxel in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer by Disease Volume and Risk Subgroups in the Phase III ARASENS Trial
Trial Name.reasoning: Extracted from the title of the document on page 1, section 83fc8176-8f32-4f0f-bbf8-972314a9e74d.

Author.reasoning: Extracted from the author list on the first page of the document, section 2f1a3239-45bc-4677-9bfc-8ab0f5b85662.

Year.reasoning: Found the copyright year '© 2023' and publication date 'February 16, 2023' on page 1, sections b85f7e7e-5aa8-4208-9e96-1d792f523d15 and 4e9cddc0-9ccb-40d0-9110-7a60d31d937e.

Full Pub or Abstract.reasoning: The document contains detailed sections such as Purpose, Methods, Results, Conclusion, Introduction, and Discussion, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.reasoning: Explicitly stated in the title of the document: 'Phase III ARASENS Trial' on page 1, section 83fc8176-8f32-4f0f-bbf8-972314a9e74d.

Original/Follow Up.reasoning: The document is explicitly described as presenting 'subgroup analyses' and 'post hoc' analyses of the Phase III ARASENS trial, as stated in the 'original reports' section, 'PURPOSE', 'CONTEXT', and 'METHODS' sections (e.g., page 1, section 83fc8176-8f32-4f0f-bbf8-972314a9e74d; page 2, section c17e53e7-e1d4-4573-93ab-7dd4a51fd215).

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.reasoning: The median treatment duration for darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel in ARASENS was reported as '41 months' in the 'DISCUSSION' section on page 11, first paragraph.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.reasoning: The overall median on-treatment duration for the placebo arm in the ARASENS trial is not explicitly reported in the document. While subgroup durations are mentioned on page 4 (e.g., 15.3 months for high-volume, 26.5 months for low-volume), an overall median for the control arm is not provided.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.reasoning: The 'METHODS' section (2e750784-9881-4b5e-8c78-9a2394ec3a6d) states that patients were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo 'plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. The 'DISCUSSION' section (a06dea29-58d0-41c9-8969-c4f33a227b2b) explicitly confirms this by stating, 'The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel'. Therefore, 100% of patients in the darolutamide (treatment) arm received docetaxel. The number of patients in the darolutamide arm for the safety analysis set is 652, as per Table 3 (8-h).

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.reasoning: The 'METHODS' section (2e750784-9881-4b5e-8c78-9a2394ec3a6d) states that patients were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo 'plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. The 'DISCUSSION' section (a06dea29-58d0-41c9-8969-c4f33a227b2b) explicitly confirms this by stating, 'The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel'. Therefore, 100% of patients in the placebo (control) arm received docetaxel. The number of patients in the placebo arm for the safety analysis set is 650, as per Table 3 (8-i) and its footnote (8-c) which clarifies that three patients in the placebo group never received assigned treatment and one patient assigned to placebo received darolutamide, leading to 650 patients in the placebo safety analysis set.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.reasoning: The 'CONTEXT' section (ID d64a3628-d114-420a-b774-cc0667f8cadd) defines 'de novo or synchronous disease' as the presence of metastatic disease at initial diagnosis. The 'CONTEXT' section (ID c17e53e7-e1d4-4573-93ab-7dd4a51fd215) and 'Primary End Point' section (ID a8021db8-d218-4f1f-83a5-a307939bc060) explicitly state that the treatment effect on OS was favorable in patients with 'de novo' metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, indicating reporting by this prognostic group.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.reasoning: The 'CONTEXT' section (ID d64a3628-d114-420a-b774-cc0667f8cadd) defines 'recurrent or metachronous disease' as developing metastases after local therapy. The 'CONTEXT' section (ID c17e53e7-e1d4-4573-93ab-7dd4a51fd215) and 'Primary End Point' section (ID a8021db8-d218-4f1f-83a5-a307939bc060) explicitly state that the treatment effect on OS was favorable in patients with 'recurrent' metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, indicating reporting by this prognostic group.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.reasoning: The 'PURPOSE' section (ID 4b374191-f050-4b5f-9316-906582918e9f) states 'We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.' The 'RESULTS' section (ID be6be8f9-6a80-4c03-a1e4-df319ac7507a) reports that 'Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume'. This is further supported by 'FIG 2. OS in subgroups of patients by (A) high-volume disease' (ID 519a093e-cae8-43fc-9ae6-802428b82a0c).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.reasoning: The 'PURPOSE' section (ID 4b374191-f050-4b5f-9316-906582918e9f) states 'We examined efficacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.' The 'RESULTS' section (ID be6be8f9-6a80-4c03-a1e4-df319ac7507a) reports that 'in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit'. This is further supported by 'FIG 2. OS in subgroups of patients by (B) low-volume disease' (ID 519a093e-cae8-43fc-9ae6-802428b82a0c).
